chapter_title.en: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca

text: 3010 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ نَاجِيَةَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِذَا خَرَجَ اَلرَّجُلُ مِنْ مِنًى أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ فَلاَ يَنْتَصِفْ لَهُ اَللَّيْلُ إِلاَّ وَ هُوَ بِمِنًى وَ إِذَا خَرَجَ بَعْدَ نِصْفِ اَللَّيْلِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُصْبِحَ بِغَيْرِهَا».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3010 - Ja'far ibn Najiya narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"If a man leaves Mina at the beginning of the night, then the night should not reach its midpoint except while he is still in Mina. However, if he leaves after midnight, then it is permissible for him to spend the morning elsewhere."
